compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome is in a state of advanced decay. The white rabbit anchor is missing from the current frame. P1, P2, P3, and P4 are no longer identifiable as living specimens.

plant_audit.p1_yellow_pot: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.p2_black_pot: Contains only debris (eggshells); no plant matter.
plant_audit.p3_black_pot: White rabbit anchor removed; previous foliage has withered into non-existence.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and compacted across all containers.
biome_observations.debris: Persistent presence of crushed eggshells, likely intended as calcium amendment, now serving as the only structural element in the pots.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of an electronic component (IC chip/header) near the pots, indicating a non-botanical environment.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state showed a single green leaf and the rabbit anchor.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Gradual loss of leaf turgor; the leaf in the black pot shows progressive yellowing and chlorosis.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: Total collapse. The specimen has either been removed or has completely desiccated/decomposed. The rabbit anchor has been removed from the scene.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is effectively dead. There is zero photosynthetic activity. The environment is currently a collection of empty pots and inert debris.
anomalies: Removal of the white rabbit scale anchor.
Introduction of electronic debris (IC chip) on the desk surface.
Complete disappearance of all biological specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical sequence documents a terminal decline. Starting from a state of minimal viability, the plants have undergone total senescence. The removal of the scale anchor and the presence of electronic waste suggest the desk is no longer being maintained as a functional botanical space.
